evidence: contextIdForProviderFinal (packages/voice-stt-deepgram/src/index.ts:473-477) returns pendingProviderFinalizeContextIds[0] (oldest pending) only when (flags.speechFinal || flags.fromFinalize). The premise that the oldest-pending entry is WRONG for the arriving final is backwards. pendingProviderFinalizeContextIds is appended in finalize-request order (trackPendingProviderFinalize, line 479-483, called only from requestProviderFinalize, line 302). Deepgram runs a single ordered WebSocket: audio for turn A is sent before turn B, the `Finalize` for A is sent before B's (line 305), and Deepgram flushes/emits A's from_finalize response before B's. So with pending [A,B], the first arriving final IS A's, and [0]=A is the CORRECT attribution, not a misattribution. This is the documented, tested design: index.test.ts:662 'correlates a pending Finalize response to the requested context across turn.change' and index.test.ts:732 both assert that a delayed finalize response is attributed back to the requesting (older) turn AFTER currentContextId has advanced via turn.change. A stale [0] cannot indefinitely absorb a later turn's final: every dequeue path clears it (resolveProviderFinalize at line 267/370/391 -> removePendingProviderFinalize line 504; timeout fallback pushFinal line 335->370; discardUnconfirmedTurn line 360; correlation-expiry timer line 495). For B's final to be misattributed to A, B's final would have to overtake A's flushed final on the wire, which an ordered socket prevents; any lost/stalled A is bounded and dequeued by providerFinalizeTimeoutMs before that.
real_fix: No code change required. FIFO [0] is the correct correlation given Deepgram's in-order emission on a single socket; there is no per-result turn token to correlate against, so ordering is the only available signal and FIFO uses it correctly. Kimi's alternative ('correlate only on fromFinalize') would REGRESS: it would drop attribution of natural speech_final responses for a turn that was finalized and then crossed turn.change (covered by index.test.ts:662 path where speechFinal can be the carrier). If extra defense-in-depth is ever wanted, the only sound hardening is to keep the per-context providerFinalizeTimeoutMs tight so a stalled oldest entry is dequeued promptly — already implemented (lines 314-318, 489-498). Recommend downgrading from high to low/none.
